trimNote: Re-recorded from scratch (2026-07-12) after the practice CSV's artist names were replaced — the original recording named real Radio Milwaukee artists next to invented spin counts, which we don't publish. Sanitized the recording scratch-directory absolute path down to station-work/ — noise removal, not content. The folder listing and full 12-row CSV dump were shortened to one-line summaries since the ranked list in the final turn already restates every track.

turns.text: There is one spreadsheet in this folder — candidates for Tuesday's adds meeting. Read it, then give me a ranked list for the meeting: which tracks should we talk about first. Flag every local artist clearly, since those get priority in our rotation policy. Don't recommend actually adding anything — that's a human call in the meeting — just help me walk in prepared.
I'll look at what's in the folder and read the spreadsheet.

tool.name: Read
tool.result: 12 rows, 8 columns — artist, track, label, release_date, local, genre, spins_last_week, notes.

turns.text:
I read the file. One thing to flag before anything else: **this is not real data.** The file is named `music-adds-SYNTHETIC.csv`, and every artist name is a placeholder — "Sample Sunroom," "Example Static," "Placeholder Neon," "Anytown Afterglow." There's no real music in here. So treat what follows as a dry run of the prep, not something you can carry into Tuesday's meeting. If there's a real candidate list somewhere else, point me at it and I'll redo this properly.

With that said, here's the ranked walkthrough of what's in the file, so you can see the shape of it.

## How I ranked it

Two things drive the order: **local artists first** (your rotation policy), then **urgency** — a track that's already out or drops on meeting day needs a decision Tuesday, while one that doesn't come out until the 21st can wait a week without cost. Spin counts are a tiebreaker, not a ranking factor, since I don't know how your policy weighs them.

## Talk about these first — LOCAL, and time-sensitive

All four are **local (MKE)** and are either already released or drop on meeting day, so a "let's revisit next week" costs you real airtime.

1. **Placeholder Neon — "Neon Hymn"** (hip-hop, out July 8, 12 spins) — 🎙️ LOCAL. Out for four days already and has the most spins of any local track. The clearest local case in the file.
2. **Sample Sunroom — "Milwaukee Sun"** (alt, releases July 14, 0 spins) — 🎙️ LOCAL. Drops the day of the meeting. Zero spins simply because it isn't out yet — don't read that as weak.
3. **Anytown Afterglow — "Sideways"** (alt, releases July 14, 3 spins) — 🎙️ LOCAL. Also a meeting-day release. Same timing pressure as Sunroom.
4. **Example Halfmoon — "Half Light"** (electronic, out July 11, 1 spin) — 🎙️ LOCAL. Out but barely moving. Probably the shortest conversation of the four, but it's local, so it gets the airtime.

## Local, but can wait if you're short on time

5. **Example Static — "Open Wide"** (hip-hop, releases July 17, 8 spins) — 🎙️ LOCAL. The 8 spins are interesting because the track isn't out yet — worth asking where those came from.
6. **Sample Ghost Parade — "Cold Static"** (electronic, releases July 21, 4 spins) — 🎙️ LOCAL. Nine days out. Genuinely fine to hold for next week's meeting.

## Non-local — get to these if time allows

7. **Placeholder Overcoat — "Overpass"** (Merge, soul, out July 2, 14 spins) — highest spin count in the entire file and already circulating.
8. **Anytown Nightshade — "Tin Roof"** (Dead Oceans, hip-hop, releases July 19, 10 spins)
9. **Example Radiogram — "Paper Radio"** (Sub Pop, indie, out July 12, 7 spins)
10. **Example Driftwood — "Signal Fade"** (Matador, hip-hop, releases July 15, 4 spins)
11. **Sample Fieldnote — "Slow Bloom"** (4AD, hip-hop, out July 6, 3 spins)
12. **Sample Bluebird — "Anyhow"** (Anti-, hip-hop, out July 12, 0 spins) — out as of today with nothing behind it yet.

## Two things worth raising in the room

**The genre mix is lopsided.** Half the list — 6 of 12 — is hip-hop. The rest is 2 alt, 2 electronic, 1 indie, 1 soul. If the meeting adds purely on individual merit, you could easily walk out having skewed the week hard in one direction. Might be worth someone naming that out loud early.

**Some spin counts predate release.** Example Static (releases the 17th) shows 8 spins, and Anytown Nightshade (releases the 19th) shows 10. That could mean advance/promo play, or it could be a data-entry problem in whatever generates this sheet. Either way I'd ask rather than assume, because those numbers are doing a lot of work in how a track reads on the page.

I've deliberately not recommended adding anything — the ranking above is only about what deserves meeting time and in what order.
